_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-11R Adenosine (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-11R: Adenosine — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) — narrow complex, regular rhythm
contraindications: Sinus node dysfunction (sick sinus syndrome) without pacemaker
High-degree AV block (2nd degree Mobitz II or 3rd degree) without pacemaker
Asthma or reactive airway disease (relative contraindication — use with caution; may cause severe bronchospasm)
precautions: Must be administered as rapid IV push followed immediately by NS flush — half-life is extremely short (~6 seconds)
Use most proximal IV site available (antecubital or above preferred); peripheral distal IV will often not work
Monitor ECG continuously during and after administration — expect brief asystole or high-degree AV block before conversion
Warn patient before administration: will feel brief intense chest pain/pressure, flushing, sense of doom — this is normal and transient
Caffeine antagonizes adenosine — higher doses may be needed in patients who regularly consume caffeine
Dipyridamole potentiates adenosine — reduce dose if patient is on dipyridamole
expected_effects: Brief AV node block interrupting re-entry circuit causing SVT
Expected: transient asystole or bradycardia followed by return of sinus rhythm
Full effect within seconds of administration
side_effects: Transient chest tightness, flushing, dyspnea, headache
Ventricular ectopy during AV block period
notes: Technique critical: rapid IV push + immediate NS flush in one motion using two-syringe technique at proximal site
Brief asystole is the mechanism of action — expected and transient; warn patient
Dipyridamole (Persantine) significantly potentiates adenosine — inform MC if patient takes it

dosing.dose: 6 mg first dose; 12 mg second dose if no conversion after 1-2 minutes
dosing.notes: Rapid IV push; immediately follow with 10-20 mL NS flush; use proximal IV site

dosing.dose: 0.1 mg/kg (max 6 mg) first dose; 0.2 mg/kg (max 12 mg) second dose if no conversion
dosing.notes: Rapid IV push; immediately follow with NS flush; proximal site required
